# Interface Contracts: Structured Roadmap Planning Layer

**Purpose**: Single source of truth for every schema, helper signature, workflow contract, and heuristic that the structured-roadmap implementation produces. Both implementers (impl-roadmap and impl-integration) MUST match these signatures and shapes exactly. Any change requires updating this file FIRST and re-notifying both implementers.

**Generated by**: `/plan` (Phase 1 — Design & Contracts)
**Consumed by**: `/implement` (impl-roadmap + impl-integration tasks must match these signatures)

## Rules (NON-NEGOTIABLE — Constitution Article VII)

- Every Bash helper script in `plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/` MUST match the signature defined in §2.
- Every JSON shape (workflow input/output, frontmatter parse output) MUST match the schema in §3.
- Every frontmatter field MUST match the schema in §1 — no extra fields, no missing required fields.
- The forbidden-sizing-fields list in §4.6 is enforced by validators; do NOT add fields without updating this file.
- Async vs sync: ALL Bash helpers are synchronous (no `&`-backgrounded operations except where explicitly noted).

---

## §1 Frontmatter Schemas

### §1.1 Vision frontmatter (`.kiln/vision.md`)

Optional — vision file may have no frontmatter. If present:

```yaml
---
last_updated: <ISO-8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD>
---
```

### §1.2 Phase frontmatter (`.kiln/roadmap/phases/<name>.md`)

Required keys (FR-005):

```yaml
---
name: <string>                 # required — matches filename sans .md
status: planned | in-progress | complete   # required
order: <integer>               # required — lower is earlier
started: <YYYY-MM-DD>          # optional — set when status → in-progress
completed: <YYYY-MM-DD>        # optional — set when status → complete
---
```

Body shape (FR-006):

```markdown
# <name>

<short description — author-edited>

## Items

- <item-id>
- <item-id>
```

The `## Items` list is **auto-maintained** by `update-phase-status.sh` and the capture skill — implementers MUST NOT hand-edit it.

### §1.3 Item frontmatter (`.kiln/roadmap/items/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md`)

Required keys (FR-007 + FR-008):

```yaml
---
id: <YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>        # required — matches filename sans .md
title: <string>                # required
kind: feature | goal | research | constraint | non-goal | milestone | critique  # required (FR-010)
date: <YYYY-MM-DD>             # required — capture date
status: <see §1.4>             # required
phase: <phase-name>            # required — must match an existing phase file
state: planned | in-phase | distilled | specced | shipped  # required (FR-021)
blast_radius: isolated | feature | cross-cutting | infra   # required (FR-008)
review_cost: trivial | moderate | careful | expert         # required (FR-008)
context_cost: <free-text rough estimate>                   # required (FR-008)
---
```

Optional keys (FR-009):

```yaml
depends_on: [<item-id>, ...]
addresses: [<critique-id>, ...]
implementation_hints: |
  <multi-line free text — flows into PRD per FR-027>
prd: <docs/features/.../PRD.md path>     # written by distill on promotion (FR-026)
spec: <specs/.../spec.md path>           # written by /specify on state: specced (FR-034)
```

Required-conditional (FR-011): if `kind: critique`, the file MUST also include:

```yaml
proof_path: <free-text — what would need to ship/measure to invalidate this critique>
```

### §1.4 `status` value sets per `kind`

| `kind` | allowed `status` values |
|--------|------------------------|
| feature | `open | in-progress | shipped | dropped` |
| goal | `open | met | dropped` |
| research | `open | in-progress | concluded | dropped` |
| constraint | `active | retired` |
| non-goal | `active | retired` |
| milestone | `pending | reached | missed` |
| critique | `open | partially-disproved | disproved` (FR-011) |

`state` is independent of `kind` and MUST be one of the FR-021 values.

### §1.5 Forbidden frontmatter keys (FR-008, schema-enforced)

The validator MUST reject any item file containing any of these top-level keys:

- `human_time`
- `human_days`
- `effort_days`
- `effort_hours`
- `t_shirt_size`
- `tshirt`
- `size` (when value matches `XS|S|M|L|XL|XXL` case-insensitive)
- `estimate_days`
- `estimate_hours`
- `pomodoros`

Rationale: AI-native sizing only. See PRD Absolute Must #3.

---

## §2 Bash Helper Signatures (`plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/`)

All helpers exit non-zero on failure with a single-line error to stderr. All helpers are deterministic (idempotent inputs → byte-identical outputs) per FR-037.

### §2.1 `parse-item-frontmatter.sh <path>`

```
Input:  $1 = path to a .kiln/roadmap/items/*.md file
Output: stdout = JSON object of frontmatter (all keys preserved verbatim)
Exit:   0 on success; 2 if file missing; 3 if frontmatter malformed
```

Example:

```json
{"id":"2026-04-24-batch-x","title":"Batch X","kind":"feature","date":"2026-04-24","status":"open","phase":"current","state":"in-phase","blast_radius":"feature","review_cost":"moderate","context_cost":"2 sessions"}
```

### §2.2 `validate-item-frontmatter.sh <path>`

```
Input:  $1 = path to a .kiln/roadmap/items/*.md file
Output: stdout = JSON {"ok": true|false, "errors": [<string>...]}
Exit:   0 always (validation result is in JSON, not exit code, so callers can batch)
```

Validation rules:

- All required keys present per §1.3.
- `kind` ∈ allowed set (§1.3).
- `status` ∈ kind-specific set (§1.4).
- `state` ∈ FR-021 set.
- Sizing fields all present and ∈ their allowed sets.
- `id` matches `^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[a-z0-9-]+$` AND equals filename sans `.md`.
- If `kind: critique`, `proof_path` present and non-empty.
- ZERO of the §1.5 forbidden keys present.

### §2.3 `validate-phase-frontmatter.sh <path>`

```
Input:  $1 = path to a .kiln/roadmap/phases/*.md file
Output: stdout = JSON {"ok": true|false, "errors": [<string>...]}
Exit:   0 always
```

Validation rules: §1.2 required keys present; `status` ∈ {planned, in-progress, complete}; `order` is integer; if `started`/`completed` present, ISO date format.

### §2.4 `list-items.sh [--phase <name>] [--kind <kind>] [--addresses <id>] [--state <state>]`

```
Input:  zero or more filters
Output: stdout = newline-separated list of repo-relative item paths, sorted ASC
Exit:   0 on success
```

Filters are AND-combined. Empty result is exit 0 with empty stdout.

### §2.5 `update-item-state.sh <path> <new-state>`

```
Input:  $1 = item path; $2 = one of {planned,in-phase,distilled,specced,shipped}
Output: stdout = JSON {"ok": true|false, "old_state": <string>, "new_state": <string>}
Exit:   0 on success; 2 if file missing; 4 if invalid state
```

MUST be atomic (write to temp file, then `mv`). MUST NOT touch any other frontmatter key.

### §2.6 `update-phase-status.sh <phase-name> <new-status> [--cascade-items]`

```
Input:  $1 = phase name; $2 = one of {planned,in-progress,complete}; --cascade-items optional
Output: stdout = JSON {"ok": true|false, "old_status": <string>, "new_status": <string>, "items_transitioned": <int>}
Exit:   0 on success; 2 if phase missing; 5 if attempting to start while another is in-progress (FR-020)
```

If `--cascade-items` and new-status is `in-progress`: every item assigned to this phase whose `state == "planned"` is updated to `state: in-phase`. (FR-021)

### §2.7 `migrate-legacy-roadmap.sh`

```
Input:  none — reads .kiln/roadmap.md if present
Output: stdout = JSON {"migrated": <int>, "skipped": <bool>, "reason": <string>}
Exit:   0 on success; 0 with skipped=true on no-op (idempotency)
```

Per FR-028: parses bullets under `## ` theme groups, writes one item file per bullet with `kind: feature`, `phase: unsorted`, `state: planned`, theme name preserved in body. Renames `.kiln/roadmap.md` to `.kiln/roadmap.legacy.md`. Skips entirely if `.kiln/roadmap.legacy.md` already exists.

### §2.8 `seed-critiques.sh`

```
Input:  none
Output: stdout = JSON {"created": <int>, "skipped": <bool>}
Exit:   0 on success
```

Per FR-029: creates three critique files only if `.kiln/roadmap/items/` is empty. The three critiques (id stable so tests can pin them):

- `2026-04-24-too-many-tokens.md` — "kiln uses too many tokens compared to doing it by hand"
- `2026-04-24-unauditable-buggy-code.md` — "kiln produces unauditable buggy code"
- `2026-04-24-too-much-setup.md` — "kiln requires too much setup"

Each ships with `proof_path` pre-filled (see §6 for exact text).

### §2.9 `classify-description.sh <description>`

```
Input:  $1 = the user's free-text description
Output: stdout = JSON {"surface": "roadmap"|"issue"|"feedback"|"ambiguous", "kind": <string|null>, "confidence": "high"|"medium"|"low"}
Exit:   0 on success
```

Cross-surface routing (FR-014) is decided FIRST — see §5 for the heuristic table. If `surface == "roadmap"`, `kind` is the auto-detected roadmap kind (FR-014a) per §4. If `surface == "ambiguous"`, both `kind` and the suggested non-roadmap surface options are returned in extended fields (`alternatives: [...]`).

### §2.10 `detect-multi-item.sh <description>`

```
Input:  $1 = the user's free-text description
Output: stdout = JSON {"is_multi": <bool>, "items": [<string>, ...]}
Exit:   0 on success
```

Detection rules (FR-018a):

- Bullet list (lines starting with `- ` / `* `): each bullet is one item.
- Numbered list (`1. `, `2. `): each entry is one item.
- "and also" / "plus" / "as well as" between concrete thing-to-build phrases: split on the conjunction.
- Newline-separated sentences where each parses as an actionable phrase: split per line.
- Fallback: `is_multi: false`, `items: [<full description>]`.

### §2.11 `parse-roadmap-input.sh` (lives in `plugin-shelf/scripts/`)

```
Input:  reads from a context block written by the calling skill (env var $ROADMAP_INPUT_BLOCK or stdin)
Output: stdout = JSON {"source_file": <string>, "basename": <string>, "frontmatter": <object>, "body": <string>, "obsidian_subpath": <string>}
Exit:   0 on success; non-zero on parse failure
```

`obsidian_subpath` is one of: `vision.md`, `roadmap/phases/<basename>`, `roadmap/items/<basename>`. The shelf workflow uses this verbatim to compose the final `<base_path>/<slug>/<obsidian_subpath>` path.

---

## §3 `shelf-write-roadmap-note` Workflow Contract

**Path**: `plugin-shelf/workflows/shelf-write-roadmap-note.json`

**Steps** (mirrors `shelf-write-issue-note` exactly):

| # | id | type | command / role |
|---|----|------|----------------|
| 1 | `read-shelf-config` | command | `bash "${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR}/scripts/parse-shelf-config.sh"` (REUSE existing helper) |
| 2 | `parse-roadmap-input` | command | `bash "${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR}/scripts/parse-roadmap-input.sh"` (NEW per §2.11) |
| 3 | `obsidian-write` | agent | composes path + writes ONE Obsidian note via MCP |
| 4 | `finalize-result` | command | jq-validates + emits final result JSON |

### §3.1 Step 3 (`obsidian-write`) MCP scope

May ONLY call:

- `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__create_file`
- `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__patch_file`

MUST NOT call: `list_files`, `update_file`, `read_file`, `search_files`, append_*, batch_*, or ANY `*-manifest__*` tool. MUST NOT invoke any other workflow.

### §3.2 Path composition decision rule (mirrors `shelf-write-issue-note` §Step 2)

```
IF shelf_config_present == "true" AND slug != "" AND base_path != "":
  path_source = ".shelf-config (base_path + slug)"
  target_path = "${base_path}/${slug}/${obsidian_subpath}"
ELSE:
  path_source = "discovery (shelf-config incomplete)"
  slug      = $(basename of git remote, .git stripped) ; fallback to "$(basename $(pwd))"
  base_path = "projects"
  target_path = "${base_path}/${slug}/${obsidian_subpath}"
```

`obsidian_subpath` is taken verbatim from `parse-roadmap-input.sh` output.

`path_source` is one of EXACTLY two literal strings (no variations):

- `".shelf-config (base_path + slug)"`
- `"discovery (shelf-config incomplete)"`

The `discovery` branch MUST NOT call `list_files` (per FR-004 — discovery here means "fall back to derived defaults", not vault listing).

### §3.3 Action selection (FR-031)

| File kind | First write | Subsequent write |
|-----------|-------------|------------------|
| `vision.md` | `create_file` | `patch_file` (frontmatter-preserving) |
| `roadmap/phases/<name>.md` | `create_file` | `patch_file` |
| `roadmap/items/<basename>` | `create_file` | `patch_file` |

Cold-start collision (create returns "already exists" / 409): fall back to `patch_file` with the SAME shape but NO inferred fields (mirrors `shelf-write-issue-note` §4 fallback rule).

### §3.4 Step 4 (`finalize-result`) JSON contract

`/wheel/outputs/shelf-write-roadmap-note-result.json`:

Success:

```json
{
  "source_file": "<repo-relative .kiln path>",
  "obsidian_path": "<target path written>",
  "action": "created" | "patched",
  "path_source": ".shelf-config (base_path + slug)" | "discovery (shelf-config incomplete)",
  "errors": []
}
```

Error:

```json
{
  "source_file": "<path or empty>",
  "obsidian_path": "<target or empty>",
  "action": "failed",
  "path_source": "unknown",
  "errors": ["<short diagnostic>"]
}
```

`path_source` MUST be present in every result (one of three literals — `unknown` only on failure).

---

## §4 Kind Auto-Detection Heuristic (FR-014a)

`classify-description.sh` returns `kind` based on this table (first match wins):

| Pattern (regex, case-insensitive) | → kind |
|-----------------------------------|--------|
| `\b(too many|too few|too slow|too expensive|broken compared|worse than|kiln (uses|produces|requires|forces) [a-z ]+(too|more|less))\b` | `critique` |
| `^(investigate|research|spike|explore|figure out)\b` | `research` |
| `^(we will not|we won't|don't [a-z]+|never [a-z]+|no [a-z]+ in v\d)\b` | `non-goal` |
| `^(constraint:|always [a-z]+|must always|always must)\b` | `constraint` |
| `^(milestone:|reached|completed phase)\b` | `milestone` |
| `\b(achieve|reduce|increase|improve [a-z]+ by)\b` (outcome framing) | `goal` |
| (default) | `feature` |

If no pattern matches with confidence, `kind: feature` is the default — but the interview asks the user to confirm if confidence is `medium` or `low`.

---

## §5 Cross-Surface Routing Heuristic (FR-014)

`classify-description.sh` runs THIS table BEFORE the §4 kind table:

| Pattern (regex, case-insensitive) | → surface |
|-----------------------------------|----------|
| `\b(broken|crashes|hangs|doesn't work|fails|errors out|wrong output|slow|stuck)\b` AND no product-intent verb | `issue` |
| `\b(should|the product should|direction|mission|architecture|scope|ergonomics)\b` AND no concrete `add\|build\|investigate` | `feedback` |
| `^(add|build|investigate|prove|disprove|we will not|kiln (should|uses|produces))` | `roadmap` |
| (anything else with neither tactical nor strategic dominance) | `ambiguous` (present all three) |

Confidence:

- `high`: only one table row matched with a strong cue (e.g., explicit "broken").
- `medium`: matched but with weak cue (e.g., "slow" alone).
- `low`: multiple rows matched or none matched.

User-facing prompt for `ambiguous` / `low`-confidence (FR-014, FR-014b):

```
This description could go to one of three places. Which captures your intent best?
  (a) /kiln:kiln-roadmap   — product intent (build / investigate / steer)
  (b) /kiln:kiln-report-issue — bug or friction with something that exists
  (c) /kiln:kiln-feedback     — strategic note about direction or scope

Pick (a) / (b) / (c):
```

Hand-off (FR-014b): on (b) or (c), the skill calls `Skill({skill: "kiln:kiln-report-issue", args: "<original description>"})` (or `kiln:kiln-feedback`) and exits. Test asserts skill invocation, NOT printed text (FR-036).

---

## §6 Adversarial Interview Question Banks (FR-015)

Each kind has ≤5 questions. Each question is individually skippable (user types `skip` or empty). The skill stops asking the moment `skip` is hit IF the rest are optional; required fields (e.g., `proof_path` for critiques) re-prompt.

### §6.1 `kind: feature`

1. What's the hardest part?
2. What are you assuming will be true?
3. What does this depend on (other items, infra, decisions)?
4. Is there a cheaper version that delivers 80% of the value?
5. What breaks if a dependency isn't ready when you start?

### §6.2 `kind: critique`

1. **`proof_path` (REQUIRED)**: What would need to ship or be measured to make this critique false?
2. Who would make this claim?
3. What items already address this critique (so we can link `addresses:`)?
4. Is this a hard critique (architectural) or a soft critique (UX friction)?
5. What's the deadline — when must we have started disproving this?

### §6.3 `kind: research`

1. What's the decision this unblocks?
2. What's the time-box (how long are you willing to spend before deciding "concluded")?
3. What does "done" look like — a doc, a prototype, a number?
4. Who's the audience for the conclusion?
5. What's the cheapest way to get a directional answer first?

### §6.4 `kind: goal`

1. How will you measure this — what's the target metric and number?
2. Which features should ladder up to this goal (link via item-id)?
3. What's the timeframe?
4. Who benefits if this is met?
5. What would convince you the goal is wrong and should be dropped?

### §6.5 `kind: constraint`, `kind: non-goal`

1. Why this constraint / non-goal? (REQUIRED — recorded as rationale in body.)
2. What would need to change for this to be revisited?
3. What items would violate this if we built them naively?

### §6.6 `kind: milestone`

1. What signals "reached"?
2. Which items must complete before this milestone fires?
3. What's the natural date if all goes well?

### §6.7 Sizing questions (asked for ALL kinds except `non-goal` / `constraint` / `milestone`)

1. **`blast_radius`** — `isolated | feature | cross-cutting | infra`?
2. **`review_cost`** — `trivial | moderate | careful | expert`?
3. **`context_cost`** — rough estimate (e.g., "1 session", "3 sessions", "one-shot")?

The interview NEVER asks for human-time or T-shirt sizes. The skill MUST refuse to write any item file whose frontmatter contains forbidden fields (validator §2.2 enforces this even if the interview is bypassed).

---

## §7 Distill Extension Contract

### §7.1 Input streams (FR-023, FR-024)

```
items_glob    = ".kiln/roadmap/items/*.md" filtered by --phase / --addresses / --kind / status≠"shipped"
feedback_glob = ".kiln/feedback/*.md" with status == "open" (existing)
issues_glob   = ".kiln/issues/*.md" (top-level only) with status == "open" (existing)
```

### §7.2 Sort order extension (FR-024 + FR-037)

Frontmatter `derived_from:` list ordering:

1. Feedback entries (filename ASC) — preserves existing FR-012 from `kiln-distill`.
2. Item entries (filename ASC) — NEW.
3. Issue entries (filename ASC) — preserves existing FR-012.

This three-group sort is the determinism hook; second distill run on unchanged inputs MUST emit byte-identical frontmatter.

### §7.3 New CLI filters (FR-025)

```
/kiln:kiln-distill --phase <name>        # default: current ; "current" = the one phase with status:in-progress
/kiln:kiln-distill --addresses <id>      # bundle items whose addresses[] contains <id> (+ feedback/issues matching same theme tag if applicable)
/kiln:kiln-distill --kind <kind>         # bundle items of one kind only
```

Filters are OR-combinable on the command line (`--phase current --kind research` = items in current phase with kind=research). When `--addresses` is given, items are the primary filter; feedback/issues join only if their `addresses` or `area` field matches the same critique-id (forward-compat — feedback may not carry `addresses` in v1; matching is best-effort).

### §7.4 PRD-side changes

- `## Background` paragraph 2 (when items present): "Recently the roadmap surfaced these items in the current phase: [list]." — concrete, not generic.
- New `## Implementation Hints` section (FR-027): renders `implementation_hints` from each selected item with `(from: <item-id>)` back-reference.
- `### Source Issues` table gains a `Type` column with values `feedback`, `item`, `issue` and rows sorted per §7.2.

### §7.5 State write on promotion (FR-026)

For each selected item, distill calls:

```
bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/update-item-state.sh <path> distilled
# then patches the item file to add: prd: docs/features/<date>-<slug>/PRD.md
```

The two writes happen as one atomic-ish sequence; if the second fails, the first is rolled back via `update-item-state.sh <path> in-phase`.

---

## §8 `/kiln:kiln-next` Extension Contract (FR-033)

`kiln-next` already lists in-flight pipeline state. The new section is appended:

```
### Active phase items

(empty if no phase has status:in-progress)

- 2026-04-24-batch-x  — feature  — state:in-phase  — phase:current
- 2026-04-22-token-cost  — critique — state:in-phase — phase:current
```

Pulled via `bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/list-items.sh --state in-phase`. Does not change other kiln-next behavior.

---

## §9 `/kiln:kiln-specify` Extension Contract (FR-034)

When `/specify` (or `/kiln:specify`) runs against a PRD whose frontmatter `derived_from:` contains one or more `.kiln/roadmap/items/*.md` paths, the skill (after spec.md is written) calls:

```
for item_path in $(jq -r '.derived_from[] | select(startswith(".kiln/roadmap/items/"))' <prd-frontmatter>); do
  bash plugin-kiln/scripts/roadmap/update-item-state.sh "$item_path" specced
  # also write spec: <spec.md path> to the item frontmatter
done
```

If no roadmap items are referenced, the hook is a no-op. Other `/specify` behavior is unchanged.

---

## §10 Pipeline-side conventions

- Spec directory MUST be `specs/structured-roadmap/` (FR-032). No numeric prefix.
- All implementation code that comes from these contracts MUST include an FR comment referencing both the spec FR and the PRD FR (e.g., `# FR-008 / PRD FR-008: AI-native sizing only`).
- Any contract change requires updating this file FIRST, then SendMessage to both implementers and team-lead.

---

## §11 Open contract questions (resolved here)

| Question | Resolution |
|----------|------------|
| Does `--addresses` match against feedback `area:` field? | Best-effort yes; when `area` substring-matches the critique-id slug, feedback joins. Documented as soft-match in distill SKILL.md. |
| Should `kind: critique` items appear in `/kiln:kiln-next` "active phase items"? | Yes — they're work the user actively cares about. |
| Does the seed-critiques bootstrap fire on every run or only first run? | Only when `.kiln/roadmap/items/` is empty (FR-029) — re-runs are no-ops. |
| What happens if a phase file is hand-edited to break `## Items` ordering? | `update-phase-status.sh` rewrites the section verbatim from `list-items.sh --phase <name>` output; hand edits to that section are overwritten on next phase touch. The phase description ABOVE `## Items` is preserved. |
| Can two items share the same `addresses` critique-id? | Yes — that's the entire point. `--addresses` bundles them all. |
